volumeCache._atlas: 2D atlas of a 3D volume: width=volumeSize voxels, height=volumeSize^2 (volumeSize Z-slices of volumeSize x volumeSize stacked vertically). Atlas (u,v)->(x,y,z): x=u, y=v%volumeSize, z=v/volumeSize. Defaults shown are for volumeSize=32 (32x1024).
volumeCache._role: Reserved scratch atlas (declared by definition.js textures; not bound by the simulate pass).

geoBuffer._role: GEO atlas (xyz=normal, w=depth) exposed as outputGeo for downstream lit raymarching. Not written by the simulate pass in this definition.

global_ca_state._atlas: Same atlas layout as volumeCache. Default 32x1024 at volumeSize=32; 64x4096 at 64; 128x16384 at 128.
global_ca_state._role: PERSISTENT feedback state atlas (r=alive, g=age, b=alive, a=1). global_ prefix -> pipeline double-buffers/ping-pongs it. BOTH input (stateTex) and output (color) of the simulate pass; each frame reads the previous frame's volume. isStateSurface matches (name contains 'state'/'ca_state') so it persists across frames (reference 04 §10.7).

atlas._comment: 3D volume stored as a 2D atlas. width = volumeSize, height = volumeSize^2 (volumeSize Z-slices of volumeSize x volumeSize stacked vertically). depth = volumeSize. Atlas (u,v) -> voxel (x = u, y = v % volumeSize, z = v / volumeSize). Inverse voxel->atlas wraps periodically: atlas.x = (x+volumeSize)%volumeSize, atlas.y = (y+volumeSize)%volumeSize + ((z+volumeSize)%volumeSize)*volumeSize. NOTE: this effect uses PARAM-SIZED atlases (volumeSize x volumeSize^2), NOT the fixed 64x4096 vol0..7 global surfaces.

feedback._comment: Single-pass multi-FRAME feedback sim (volume-write). State lives in ONE persistent 'global_' atlas global_ca_state (r=alive, g=age). The simulate pass reads its own previous output (stateTex == global_ca_state) and writes the next volume (color == global_ca_state); the runtime ping-pongs the surface across frames (within-frame ping-pong ref 04 §10.2, end-of-frame persistence via isStateSurface ref 04 §10.7 — name contains 'state'). seedTex ('source' volume / inputTex3d) seeds the first frame and on reset, and optionally blends in via 'weight'. No '_iteration' uniform. No repeat. No MRT (single color output). The shader self-initializes by detecting an all-zero buffer.
